title: IDisposable should be implemented correctly
description: "IDisposable" should be implemented correctlyAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoTheIDisposableinterface is a mechanism to release unmanaged resources, if not implemented correctly this could result in resource leaks or more severe bugs.This rule raises an issue when the recommended dispose pattern, as defined by Microsoft, is not adhered to. See theCompliant Solutionsection for examples.Satisfying the rule’s conditions will enable potential derived classes to correctly dispose the members of your class:sealedclasses are not checked.If a base class implementsIDisposableyour class should not haveIDisposablein the list of its interfaces. In such cases it is recommended to override the base class’sprotected virtual void Dispose
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detect classes implementing IDisposable without proper dispose pattern (missing GC.SuppressFinalize)
logic.triggering_conditions: (Class implements IDisposable) AND (Has public Dispose()) AND (Missing GC.SuppressFinalize) AND (NOT sealed)
logic.reasoning: Enforces proper IDisposable pattern. Non-sealed classes implementing IDisposable should call GC.SuppressFinalize(this). Pattern uses negative lookahead to detect Dispose() WITHOUT GC.SuppressFinalize. Excludes sealed classes and proper implementations.

should_trigger: public class MyClass : IDisposable { public void Dispose() { } }
should_not_trigger: public class MyClass : IDisposable { public void Dispose() { GC.SuppressFinalize(this); } }
public sealed class MyClass : IDisposable { public void Dispose() { } }
